eyebrowSequence: Run agents where the work happens
hero.title: Aizen
hero.subtitle: A macOS workspace for parallel development. Run agents, terminals, and browsers side by side across every branch.
hero.meta: Available now on macOS. Apple Silicon only.

why.title: What makes it different
why.subtitle: Agentic-first, parallel by default, native on macOS, and open source from the start.

native.title: Agentic-first
native.desc: Run Claude, Codex, Gemini, and other ACP agents directly where the work happens. Add MCP servers from the built-in marketplace.

modern.title: Parallel
modern.desc: Each project or branch gets its own environment with terminal panes, file tabs, browser tabs, and chat history.

sync.title: Native
sync.desc: Built for macOS with GPU-accelerated terminal, Metal rendering, and native platform integration.

pricing.title: Open source
pricing.desc: The code is public under GPL-3.0, inspectable, and developed in the open.

now.title: Core workspace
now.desc: Ship the full parallel development workspace on macOS.

next.title: Agent ecosystem
next.desc: Deeper ACP integrations and agent workflow tooling.

later.title: Platform expansion
later.desc: Bring the workspace to more platforms.

features.title: What you can do with it
features.subtitle: Manage projects, run agents, and keep every environment ready.

rendering.title: Workspace and project management
rendering.desc: Group active projects into workspaces, then create environments for the branches or tasks that are active right now.

connect.title: Run agents where the work happens
connect.desc: Run Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, and other ACP agents. Add MCP servers per agent from inside the app.

pickup.title: GPU terminal with persistence
pickup.desc: GPU-accelerated terminal powered by libghostty with split panes, presets, themes, and optional tmux persistence.

organize.title: Git, review, and CI in one place
organize.desc: Stage, commit, diff, review pull requests, and keep GitHub Actions or GitLab CI runs within reach.

useCases.title: Where it is useful
useCases.subtitle: Useful for parallel development, agent workflows, code review, and project management.

solo.title: Parallel development
solo.desc: Work across multiple branches or projects without losing your place. Each environment keeps its own state.

homelab.title: Agent workflows
homelab.desc: Run multiple agents in parallel across different environments. Watch them work while you review in another.

apple.title: Code review
apple.desc: Review pull requests with inline diffs, file browser, and terminal access in the same environment.

ai.title: Project management
ai.desc: Group projects into workspaces, track CI runs, and keep everything organized in one native app.

pricing.title: Simple pricing
pricing.subtitle: Free, Pro, and Lifetime. Use the full core app for free.

free.name: Free
free.description: The full core workspace, free forever

q1.question: Too many windows
q1.answer: Developers working across multiple projects or branches usually juggle terminals, editor windows, docs, CI dashboards, and agent chats. Aizen keeps that stack attached to each environment.

q2.question: Agents need a workspace
q2.answer: Running agents in a terminal is fine for one task. But when you have multiple agents across branches, you need a workspace that keeps them organized.

q3.question: Native matters
q3.answer: I do not want one heavy interface forced onto every platform. The app should feel right where it runs.

q4.question: Keep it useful
q4.answer: The goal is a tool that stays good at parallel development, agents, and project management instead of expanding into noise.

closing.title: Available now on macOS.
closing.subtitle: An agentic-first workspace for parallel development. Open source and free to use.

footer.description: Open source macOS workspace with project environments, terminal, files, browser, agents, workflows, and CLI.
